response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned senior developer with broad full-stack experience and notable strengths in D (a systems language with C++-like ergonomics), low-latency services at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby, and geospatial work via GISCollective and Green Map. However, the role explicitly requires modern C++ or Rust experience, and the resume shows only brief C/C++ exposure at National Instruments (2014-2015) and no Rust. Recent work is JS/TS/Node-heavy, far from a C++ routing tiling pipeline. The geospatial background and systems-programming mindset (DLang, async I/O, high-perf databases) are transferable, so a screening interview could validate whether C++ skills are current enough — but the gap against the primary requirement is significant.
strengths: Geospatial/GIS domain experience via GISCollective and Green Map
Low-latency, high-throughput systems background at Sociomantic (DLang, async I/O, fibers)
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, testability, OSS contributions
concerns: No modern C++ or Rust experience evident; only legacy C/C++ at NI a decade ago
Recent 10+ years dominated by JS/TS/Node, not systems languages
No navigation/routing pipeline or tiling experience
